howItWorks.intro: This time lapse interval calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Interval
rules.explanation: Total recording time divided by frames needed.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What interval should I use?
faq.answer: 1-2s for fast action, 5-10s for clouds, 15-30s for construction/plants.

faq.question: How many photos do I need?
faq.answer: Multiply desired video length by frame rate (e.g., 30 seconds × 30 fps = 900 frames).

faq.question: Is the Time Lapse Interval Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard photography formulas trusted by photographers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What event duration should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate event duration value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Time Lapse Interval Calculator?
sections.body: The Time Lapse Interval Calculator is a free online tool designed for photographers who need quick, accurate calculations in the photography space. By entering your event duration, desired clip length, video frame rate, you get instant results including total frames needed, shoot interval, est. storage. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total frames needed right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In photography, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by photographers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to capture better images with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your event duration and need to find the right total frames needed. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Event Duration (minutes)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your event duration in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Desired Clip Length (seconds)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your desired clip length in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Video Frame Rate (fps)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your video frame rate in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your total frames needed, shoot interval, est. storage. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your event duration before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both total frames needed and shoot interval — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to capture better images.
If you're unsure about your video frame rate, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
